content.verdict: Xigera is the Botswana camp for honeymooners who want a safari that doubles as a contemporary African art gallery. Rebuilt in 2021 on a private concession bordering Moremi Game Reserve, the lodge was developed with Cape Town's Southern Guild gallery as curator — every light fitting, every basin, every door handle and chair is a commissioned piece from an African artist. Twelve suites on raised stilts above the Delta channels are vast, entirely solar-powered, and fitted with bronze-cast sculptures and hand-woven textiles. The main areas include a wine cellar for private dinners, a wellness centre with proper spa treatment rooms, and the Baobab Treehouse for star-bed sleep-outs. The game viewing combines water and land with strong leopard, elephant and lion showings. It is the most design-forward safari lodge in Botswana — and arguably the entire continent.
content.best_room: All twelve suites are essentially identical and spectacular; pick on orientation. The suites at the eastern end of the walkway have the most dramatic sunrise exposure over the floodplain. For a once-in-a-lifetime splurge, request the Baobab Treehouse for one night — a private star-bed platform perched in an ancient baobab, served dinner and breakfast by your butler, utterly silent except for the hippos below.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and design orientation
itinerary_7_nights.description: Light-aircraft transfer from Maun onto the Xigera airstrip, champagne welcome, a guided walk through the art installations with the on-site curator, and an evening boat cruise along the channel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Leopard morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn coffee, a long morning drive into the Moremi concession tracking the resident leopards, brunch back at the main area, an afternoon plunge-pool siesta and sundowners on the floodplain.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Mokoro and wellness
itinerary_7_nights.description: A silent dawn mokoro glide with your private poler, breakfast on a quiet island, an afternoon couples treatment in the spa, and a private dinner in the wine cellar beneath the lodge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Full-day drive
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pack a picnic, head deep into the Moremi concession for a full day of game viewing with lunch in a shady grove, and return for a private bush dinner under the stars.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Baobab Treehouse night
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning drive, a leisurely afternoon at camp, transfer to the Baobab Treehouse for sundowners, dinner under the canopy and a star-bed night in one of the most romantic sleeping platforms in Africa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Boat and art
itinerary_7_nights.description: A gentle morning boat cruise with elephants along the banks, brunch, an afternoon walk through the Southern Guild art collection with the curator, and a boma dinner around the fire.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell and transfer
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final dawn drive with coffee and rusks, a long farewell brunch and a light-aircraft transfer onwards or back to Maun.

caveats: Xigera is reached only by light aircraft from Maun with the standard 20 kg soft-sided duffel limit per guest — no hard cases, no exceptions, and flight timings can move for weather.
Xigera sits within the Delta water system — May to September brings high flood and strong boat and mokoro activity, while October to April is drier and better for land-based predator drives.
This is a malaria region — consult your travel doctor about prophylaxis, bring DEET and long-sleeved clothing for dusk, and be aware of normal bush-camp insect life including the occasional tsetse.

faqs.question: How does Xigera differ from the Wilderness flagships?
faqs.answer: Xigera is privately owned by the Tollman family (the Red Carnation Hotel Collection) and curated by Southern Guild, which gives it a design-gallery feel that none of the Wilderness or Great Plains camps attempt. Game viewing is strong but the headline is the architecture and art.

faqs.question: Is the Baobab Treehouse worth it?
faqs.answer: For honeymooners, absolutely — it's one of the most spectacular sleep-out experiences in Africa. Book it in advance; only one couple per night can use it and demand is high during peak season.

faqs.question: Is the spa a real spa?
faqs.answer: Yes — Xigera runs a dedicated wellness centre with treatment rooms, a hair and nail salon, and a menu of massages and facials using high-quality African botanical products. It is more serious than most bush-camp spas.

faqs.question: Are children allowed?
faqs.answer: Xigera accepts children from six years old, with a dedicated family suite. In practice the camp feels adult-led and couples-heavy, with plenty of quiet corners for honeymooners.

faqs.question: Can we buy the art?
faqs.answer: Yes — every piece is for sale through Southern Guild's Cape Town and Los Angeles galleries. The lodge has an on-site curator who can arrange viewings, pricing and international shipping if you fall in love with something.

faqs.question: What's included in the rate?
faqs.answer: All meals, all drinks including premium wines and spirits, all scheduled activities (drives, boat, mokoro, walks), laundry, park fees, Wi-Fi throughout, and light-aircraft transfers to and from Maun.
